data.title: CACI to boost U.S. Army readiness with next-gen electronic warfare solutions
data.description:
CACI International (CACI) has been awarded a five-year task order valued at up to $250 million to enhance spectrum dominance for the U.S. Army Capability Program Executive—Intelligence, Electronic Warfare & Sensors (CPE IEW&S).

CACI will leverage its intrinsic knowledge to provide comprehensive integration and sustainment of the technology to advance the Army’s multi-domain EW and spectrum dominance capabilities.

data.title: United Airlines announces major expansion at Chicago O'Hare amid hot demand
data.description:
United Airlines Holdings (UAL) plans to increase its schedule at Chicago O’Hare International Airport to a record level for the summer. The airline is planning 750 daily flights from its home city, which is 200 more than its nearest competitor and the largest schedule ever operated by any airline at the airport. 

The carrier will offer nonstop service to 222 destinations from Chicago, including 47 international cities across Europe, Asia, and South America, and 175 U.S. destinations. United (UAL) will also strengthen regional connectivity beginning in April with new routes from ORD to Champaign/Urbana, Kalamazoo, Lansing, La Crosse, and Bloomington/Normal.

Building on its 2025 expansion, United (UAL) will also launch flights to nine additional U.S. cities, including Santa Barbara, Monterey, Eugene, and Erie, while adding frequencies to 80 existing destinations nationwide, such as Boston, Nashville, Los Angeles, and San Francisco. With these additions, ORD is set to become the third-largest airline hub in the United States. United (UAL) will briefly operate daily service to Guadalajara, Mexico, in June to accommodate travel for major international soccer events, ensuring nonstop access to all hosting cities.

Operationally, United (UAL) highlighted that it led major carriers in on-time arrivals at ORD in 2025 while operating 31% more flights than its nearest competitor. The airline plans to hire approximately 2,500 additional employees in Chicago this year and increase mainline departures by 20% over summer 2025.

United (UAL) noted that it continues to enhance its travel technology suite, with tools such as Connection Saver, which prevented one million missed connections in 2025; app-guided navigation for tight connections; and expanded use of TSA PreCheck Touchless ID and Bag Drop Shortcut services. 

Shares of UAL edged 0.4% higher in premarket trading.

data.title: Zephyr announces increase in private placement
data.description:
• Zephyr Minerals (ZFR:CA) on Tuesday announced that it has increased the size of its previously announced non-brokered private placement to an aggregate of 5,200,000 units at a price of $0.05 per unit, for gross proceeds of up to $260,000.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one common share purchase warrant of the company. Each warrant will entitle the holder to acquire one additional share at a price of $0.10 per share for a period of two years from the date of issuance.
  • Proceeds received from the private placement will be used for general working capital purposes and to advance permitting on the Dawson Gold project. It is anticipated that certain insiders of the company will participate in the private placement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Camden National Non-GAAP EPS of $1.33 beats by $0.01, revenue of $68.05M beats by $1.7M
data.description:
• Camden National press release (CAC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.33 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $68.05M (+43.0% Y/Y) beats by $1.7M.
  • For the quarter ended December 31, 2025, the Company reported a return on average assets of 1.28%, a return on average equity of 13.01%, and a return on average tangible equity (non-GAAP) of 19.06%.
  • Net interest margin for the fourth quarter of 2025 increased 13 basis points over the third quarter of 2025 to 3.29%, and core net interest margin increased 10 basis points over the same period to 2.92%.
  • GAAP efficiency ratio for the fourth quarter of 2025 was 54.16%, while the non-GAAP efficiency ratio was 51.69%.
  • Book value per share increased 3% from September 30, 2025 to $41.16 as of December 31, 2025, and tangible book value per share (non-GAAP) increased 4% during the same period to $29.69 at year-end.

data.title: Bluejay Diagnostics announces 1-for-4 reverse stock split
data.description:
Bluejay Diagnostics (BJDX) on Tuesday announced a 1-for-4 reverse stock split, effective 12:01 AM ET on January 29.

Shares were -2.66% pre-market to $0.77.

New CUSIP number for the shares will be 095633608.

The share combination will reduce the medical technology company's outstanding common stock to ~708,533 from ~2.83M.

Bluejay shareholders will receive a cash payment in lieu of the fractional shares.

data.title: Kobo Resources announces non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Kobo Resources (KRI:CA) on Tuesday intends to complete a non-brokered private placement of 958,306 common shares at a price of $0.30 per common share for gross proceeds of $287,491.80.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds of the offering for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • Closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about February 3, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Boeing beats loss forecast, shares steady as one-time gain masks ongoing risks
data.description:
Boeing (BA) shares slipped 1.5% in premarket trading Tuesday after the plane maker reported fourth quarter results that beat Wall Street expectations for earnings and revenue, with investors weighing strong delivery growth against lingering operational and financial concerns.

The company (BA) posted an adjusted profit of $9.92 a share, well ahead of the consensus estimate for an adjusted loss of $1.39 a share. Quarterly results were heavily influenced by a $9.6 billion gain tied to the sale of its Digital Aviation Solutions business, which significantly lifted reported earnings, according to Boeing (BA).

Revenue rose 57% from a year earlier to $23.95 billion, topping analyst expectations of $22.84 billion.

Net income rose to $8.22 billion, or $10.23 a share, from a loss of $3.86 billion, or $5.46 a share, a year earlier.

The company delivered 160 commercial airplanes during the quarter, reflecting improved operational performance across its production system. The 737 program lifted output to 42 a month in the quarter, while the 787 program reached eight planes a month. The company said it “remains focused on stabilizing at that rate.” Boeing (BA) booked 336 net orders in the quarter.  

President and Chief Executive Officer Kelly Ortberg said the company continued to make progress on its turnaround.

“We made significant progress on our recovery in 2025 and have set the foundation to keep our momentum going in the year ahead,” he said in prepared remarks.

Operating cash flow in the quarter totaled $1.3 billion, while free cash flow was $0.4 billion, supported by higher deliveries and working capital timing.

Underlying pressures remain

Despite the headline profit, investors appeared cautious as Boeing (BA) continued to report losses in key businesses. The commercial airplanes segment posted an operating loss of $632 million in the quarter, while defense, space & security recorded a $507 million operating loss that included $0.6 billion of charges on the KC-46A tanker program tied to higher production support and supply chain costs.

For the full year, Boeing (BA) generated negative free cash flow of $1.9 billion, highlighting the ongoing strain on cash generation even as revenue climbed to $89.5 billion. Debt also rose following the acquisition of supplier Spirit AeroSystems, with consolidated debt ending the year at $54.1 billion.

Certification timelines may also be a factor for investors. Boeing (BA) said it still expects first delivery of the 777-9 in 2027.

Backlog reaches record

Boeing (BA) ended the year with a record backlog of $682 billion, including more than 6,100 commercial airplanes, underscoring long term demand for its products even as near term execution risks persist.

data.title: PACCAR GAAP EPS of $1.06 beats by $0.01, revenue of $6.82B beats by $740M
data.description:
• PACCAR press release (PCAR): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.06 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $6.82B (-13.8% Y/Y) beats by $740M.
  • PACCAR declared cash dividends of $2.72 per share during 2025. This included a $1.40 per share year-end cash dividend paid on January 7, 2026.
  • PACCAR delivered 144,200 vehicles worldwide.
  • Record PACCAR Parts revenue of $1.74 billion.
  • PACCAR Parts pretax income of $415.0 million.
  • PACCAR Financial Services pretax income of $114.9 million.
  • Cash provided by operations of $1.14 billion.

data.title: Karman Space & Defense awarded MDA contract under $151B SHIELD IDIQ
data.description:
• Karman Space & Defense (KRMN) on Tuesday said it was awarded a contract for the Missile Defense Agency Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense (SHIELD) indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity (IDIQ) contract with a ceiling of $151B.
  • This contract encompasses a broad range of work areas that allows for the rapid delivery of innovative capabilities to the warfighter with increased speed and agility.
  • KRMN +3.94% premarket to $113.0.
  • Source: Press Release
